expected_curve: value_profile_cmplog flat-high; cmplog monotone-decreasing in DECOY_COUNT; naive ~flat

acceptance.rule: value_profile_cmplog >= naive >= cmplog at all doses; gap (winner - cmplog) grows with DECOY_COUNT; at DECOY_COUNT=8, value_profile_cmplog > 0 while cmplog is suppressed (>=30pp / clearly fewer crashes than value_profile_cmplog). If cmplog matches value_profile_cmplog at high dose, the I2S-pollution story is refuted.

design_basis: DECOY_COUNT scales the number of LIVE decoy equality gates over the input window. Each live decoy emits a distinct CMP operand into the CmpLogObserver, so more decoys = more literals I2SRandReplace can harvest and substitute into the window, which re-triggers the decoy and XOR-corrupts the derived field away from TARGET (active I2S corpus pollution). The target gradient (acc==TARGET) is a single un-substitutable full-width compare, constant across the sweep, so value_profile's CMP_MAP can always climb it; the knob therefore widens the cmplog deficit without changing the VP-climbable signal.
notes: DECOY_COUNT=1: one decoy literal; weak I2S misdirection. cmplog ~ naive ~ value_profile_cmplog (small or no gap).
DECOY_COUNT=2: two competing literals; cmplog begins to lag as I2S substitutions pull the window onto decoys.
DECOY_COUNT=4: four competing literals; cmplog's corpus is materially polluted, value_profile_cmplog still climbs the unchanged gradient, naive unaffected by CMP-directed substitution.
DECOY_COUNT=8: maximal misdirection; cmplog suppressed while value_profile_cmplog escapes via the CMP_MAP gradient and naive (no I2S) is not dragged toward decoys.

HONESTY CAVEAT: this composite (VP-helps AND I2S-hurts simultaneously) is hard to capture faithfully in a micro-harness; the decoy XOR is an explicit stand-in for the opaque derived-field corruption seen in the real targets. See feature_spec.json hypothesis.tradeoff_observation.

screen._note: screen-tuning experiment (2026-05-24): override for the fast re-screen; honest grid stays in scan_values above
